note: Real source of SecUSo Privacy Friendly Notes, pinned just before commit a580bcd ('[fix] adds trigger creation in room callback') so that commit can be edit 01 -- a genuine self-contained real changeset (14 lines, one file). Not the whole app (46 files spanning the note editor UI, sketch/audio recording, category management, backup-api) -- an acyclic real SUBGRAPH covering the distinctive behaviors this app was picked for: the real Room database (room/NoteDatabase.java + room/DbContract.java + room/dao/{NoteDao,NotificationDao,CategoryDao}.kt + room/model/{Note,Category,Notification}.kt, vendored as a complete, self-contained package -- Room annotations resolve fine against androidx.room:room-runtime without needing Room's annotation processor to actually run, since this harness only compiles, never runs kapt/ksp), AlarmManager-scheduled reminders (ui/helper/NotificationHelper.kt), and the boot-completed + exact-alarm-permission-changed receiver that reschedules all pending notifications from the DB (receiver/NotificationEventsReceiver.kt), plus the notification-display receiver that dispatches into the note-type Activities (receiver/NotificationReceiver.kt). Same acyclic-subgraph technique as pedometer/todo-list: pin real leaf files, author minimal scaffolding only where the real files reference UI they don't otherwise need -- ui/notes/{BaseNoteActivity,TextNoteActivity,AudioNoteActivity,SketchActivity,ChecklistNoteActivity}.kt are stand-in Activities (the vendored NotificationReceiver.kt only builds Intent objects targeting them, never calls into their bodies) and AndroidManifest.xml/values/styles.xml are scaffolding (no custom Application class, no backup-api service, no note-editor/category/settings activities). Excludes :backup-api and the PFNotesApplication custom Application subclass (same rationale as 2048/pedometer). Needs androidx.room:room-runtime (Room annotations + RoomDatabase/Migration/SupportSQLiteDatabase classes), androidx.core (ContextCompat), androidx.lifecycle (LiveData return types in the daos), and kotlinx-coroutines-core (Flow return types in NoteDao) on the compile classpath. Real strings.xml and the mdpi launcher/notification icons are vendored as-is (only mdpi density, same as ruler/2048/pedometer/todo-list). values/colors.xml is scaffolding (not vendored) -- it supplies R.color.colorPrimary, which the vendored NotificationReceiver.kt calls via resources.getColor() but which no version of the app's own colors.xml actually defines (the app only declares colorPrimary as a theme attr); see the file's own comment.
